title: Father Knows Worst

jokes.text: Hot dogs, not so hot dogs! Cold and droopy!
jokes.setup: Boardwalk vendor calling out food items
jokes.punchline: Honest advertising about terrible hot dogs
jokes.explanation: Strong wordplay on 'hot dogs' becoming 'not so hot dogs' with vivid description

jokes.text: Fried dough! America's worst legal food!
jokes.setup: Continuing the honest carnival food advertising
jokes.punchline: Qualifying fried dough as worst LEGAL food implies illegal foods exist
jokes.explanation: Dark implication about illegal foods plus honest carnival criticism

jokes.text: Never leaves your body! The Truth in Boardwalking Law has really done a number on this place.
jokes.setup: Continuing the honest food advertising theme
jokes.punchline: Made-up 'Truth in Boardwalking Law' as parody of truth in advertising
jokes.explanation: Clever made-up law name that sounds official but is absurd

jokes.text: Shoot an oversized basketball into an undersized hoop! It's impossible! The prizes have been here for 50 years!
jokes.setup: Carnival game operator describing his game
jokes.punchline: Admitting the game is rigged and prizes are ancient
jokes.explanation: Standard carnival scam observation but well-executed with specific details

jokes.text: I could juggle six pins if they let me use a unicycle.
jokes.setup: Bart dismissing street performer's juggling act
jokes.punchline: Absurd logic that unicycling would make juggling easier
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart logic - confident dismissal with nonsensical reasoning

jokes.text: Are you insane? You can't throw a little boy into my cascade. Stop tossing kids!
jokes.setup: Bart challenges juggler to juggle something harder
jokes.punchline: Bart apparently gets thrown into the juggling act
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from challenge to chaos, 'Stop tossing kids!' is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: Finally, I can have fun with something I bought for myself! A new water heater.
jokes.setup: Homer excited about Saturday fun time
jokes.punchline: Reveals his idea of fun is a water heater
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer characterization - domestic dad excitement over appliances

jokes.text: Five more water heaters, and we get a free water heater!
jokes.setup: Homer excited about his water heater purchase
jokes.punchline: Absurd loyalty program requiring 6 water heaters to get one free
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist economics plus Homer's consumer enthusiasm

jokes.text: It's like a sauna in here. It is a sauna, in here.
jokes.setup: Homer making observation about hot room
jokes.punchline: Corrects himself - it literally is a sauna
jokes.explanation: Clean misdirection structure, economy of language

jokes.text: My invitation didn't say nothing about no towels, so, I guess we should all just go Scandinavian.
jokes.setup: Moe arriving at Homer's sauna
jokes.punchline: Double negative grammar plus euphemism for nude sauna
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe voice with crude grammar and uncomfortable situation

jokes.text: Maybe I'll keep this to myself for a while.
jokes.setup: Homer's friends immediately invade his private sauna discovery
jokes.punchline: Homer's delayed reaction to close the barn door after horses escaped
jokes.explanation: Standard 'too late' reaction but fits the situation

jokes.text: Chicken kebab! Fire kebab! - Dad, no! - Dad, yes!
jokes.setup: Food vendor listing kebab types, Bart warns Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer's determined 'Dad, yes!' response to eating fire
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer stubbornness, great rhythm in the back-and-forth

jokes.text: Why... you... little... Burn, Barty, burn!
jokes.setup: Homer's classic strangling setup phrase
jokes.punchline: Subverts expectation with 'Burn Barty burn' (disco reference)
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of Homer's signature move with disco reference

jokes.text: It may put something of a cramp in your lovemaking. No, it won't... if he wants me to do something, he'll write it down.
jokes.setup: Doctor warning about tongue cast affecting intimacy
jokes.punchline: Marge's matter-of-fact response about written instructions
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly frank discussion of marital intimacy with practical solution

jokes.text: Is it complimentary? Yeah, sure, it's complimentary. It is? Hey, everybody, check out my compliment!
jokes.setup: Homer asking if Bart's note is complimentary
jokes.punchline: Homer misunderstands, thinks it's a compliment and shows everyone
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay on complimentary/compliment, Homer's obliviousness

jokes.text: I held up two for 'peely'! I thought that was a 'Y,' for 'Yanky.' I'll make a note in your chart that you aren't always clear.
jokes.setup: Confusion over hand signals for cast removal method
jokes.punchline: Doctor blaming Homer for miscommunication and documenting it
jokes.explanation: Good medical bureaucracy satire, escalating the miscommunication

jokes.text: Too much cherry! Lemon. That'll stop the cherry.
jokes.setup: Homer trying cherry flavoring with super taste buds
jokes.punchline: Homer's flawed logic about using lemon to counteract cherry
jokes.explanation: Homer's typical problem-solving logic making things worse

jokes.text: Well, I do have an idea, but just to be sure, let's run some expensive tests.
jokes.setup: Homer asking why taste problems are happening
jokes.punchline: Doctor admitting he knows but wants to run expensive tests anyway
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of medical system prioritizing profit over efficiency

jokes.text: That'll be our Christmas card, this year.
jokes.setup: Doctor showing Homer's burned tongue x-ray
jokes.punchline: Marge suggesting using medical imagery for Christmas card
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about medical trauma as holiday greeting

jokes.text: I can't eat tacos? Oh, my God. What day of Mayo is it? Cinco.
jokes.setup: Doctor warning Homer about spicy foods like tacos
jokes.punchline: Homer's panic about missing Cinco de Mayo ('day of Mayo')
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay on 'Mayo' plus Homer's food priorities over health

jokes.text: Try these beets. I boiled all the red out.
jokes.setup: Marge trying to help Homer with bland food
jokes.punchline: Absurd cooking method of boiling color out of vegetables
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd cooking logic, great visual of colorless beets

jokes.text: I still taste overtones of pink!
jokes.setup: Homer trying the deliberately bland beets
jokes.punchline: Homer's super-taster detecting 'overtones of pink' (color as flavor)
jokes.explanation: Brilliant synesthesia joke - tasting colors, perfectly absurd escalation

jokes.text: It feels like food, but it has no flavor whatsoever! It's mac and cheese from the school cafeteria.
jokes.setup: Lisa describing perfect bland food for Homer
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's school cafeteria mac and cheese
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff structure, universal school food experience

jokes.text: chili con cottage cheese, pecan blandies, cream of steam, you guess 'ems...
jokes.setup: Listing school cafeteria food options
jokes.punchline: Series of increasingly absurd food names ending with 'you guess 'ems'
jokes.explanation: Creative food name wordplay, escalating absurdity, 'you guess 'ems' is brilliant

jokes.text: Old comedy writers who now have to work at our kitchen. The one rule we had on Charles in Charge, is Charles must always be in charge.
jokes.setup: Explanation of who creates the absurd food names
jokes.punchline: Washed-up TV writers, with specific reference to formulaic sitcom rule
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on comedy writing, specific TV reference adds authenticity

jokes.text: I'm big and yellow and I'm servin' Jell-O. If you're a fellow and you want some Jell-O, you better bellow 'Hey, big guy, give me some Jell-O.'
jokes.setup: Homer working cafeteria job, trying not to embarrass Bart
jokes.punchline: Elaborate rhyming chant about serving Jell-O
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer embarrassing Bart moment, elaborate rhyme scheme

jokes.text: No Jell-O for you, Bart. It'll keep you up at night.
jokes.setup: Homer serving cafeteria food
jokes.punchline: Treating Jell-O like it's caffeinated
jokes.explanation: Homer's misguided parental authority over harmless dessert

jokes.text: I used to date your daughter. Buddy, I'm here to eat, not make interesting conversation.
jokes.setup: Old man trying to make conversation with Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer's blunt shutdown of social interaction
jokes.explanation: Homer's antisocial directness, good contrast with attempted friendliness

jokes.text: It's bad enough I have to be here three days a week.
jokes.setup: Bart complaining about Homer hanging out at school
jokes.punchline: Reveals Bart only goes to school three days a week
jokes.explanation: Casual revelation of Bart's truancy as side comment

jokes.text: Hi, Homer Simpson. Mind if I sit near your son? Mine sucks.
jokes.setup: Parent introducing himself to Homer
jokes.punchline: Blunt admission that his own son sucks
jokes.explanation: Shocking parental honesty, economy of language, great setup for contrast

jokes.text: Noah, state capitals: alphabetical order, on my clap! Albany! Annapolis! Atlanta! Augusta! Austin! Baton Rouge! Bismarck! Boise! Boston! Capital City! Carson City!
jokes.setup: Overachieving parent demonstrating son's abilities
jokes.punchline: Rapid-fire recitation showing excessive pressure on child
jokes.explanation: Good helicopter parent satire, child performance anxiety

jokes.text: He's terrified of you. That's why I'm here: to make sure he gets good grades.
jokes.setup: Homer observing the kid's fear
jokes.punchline: Dad admitting terror is his parenting strategy
jokes.explanation: Dark parenting philosophy stated matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Isn't that why we pay school teachers those enormous salaries?
jokes.setup: Discussion about helping kids get good grades
jokes.punchline: Homer's sarcastic comment about teacher pay
jokes.explanation: Good social commentary on teacher salaries via Homer's sarcasm

jokes.text: Well, call me over-involved, but someday, your son will be my son's chauffeur.
jokes.setup: Defending his helicopter parenting
jokes.punchline: Predicting class hierarchy with Bart as servant
jokes.explanation: Sharp class warfare commentary disguised as parenting philosophy

jokes.text: Well, I hope your son enjoys being driven around by the president of the United States.
jokes.setup: Responding to chauffeur prediction
jokes.punchline: Homer's absurd counter-claim about Bart becoming president
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer parental delusion, good escalation of the rivalry

jokes.text: Really? What the future president's doing right now.
jokes.setup: Challenging Homer's claim about Bart becoming president
jokes.punchline: Cut to Bart doing something unpresidential
jokes.explanation: Good setup for visual gag showing Bart's current behavior

jokes.text: You don't see many presidents doing that. Maybe L.B.J. at the end.
jokes.setup: Observing Bart's unpresidential behavior
jokes.punchline: Reference to LBJ's decline suggesting he might have done the same
jokes.explanation: Dark historical humor about presidential dignity, specific and clever

jokes.text: It's 'helicopter parenting.' I don't have to 'helicopter' Bart. I've got a backup plan called 'Lisa.'
jokes.setup: Explanation of helicopter parenting concept
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual admission he's given up on Bart, banking on Lisa
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark parenting honesty, treating children as investments

jokes.text: Sure, she's smart, but how can she be successful if she's not popular?
jokes.setup: Discussing Lisa as Homer's backup plan
jokes.punchline: Identifying the flaw in Homer's plan - Lisa's social status
jokes.explanation: Good observation about social vs academic success

jokes.text: Well, we're having cold shoulder.
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to sit with popular girls, asking what they're eating
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation of 'cold shoulder' treatment
jokes.explanation: Clever literalization of common phrase, perfect economy

jokes.text: My God! My son's a loser and my daughter's a loner!
jokes.setup: Homer realizing his children's social problems
jokes.punchline: Alliterative categorization of his kids' issues
jokes.explanation: Good alliteration, Homer's parental panic, economical summary

jokes.text: No time. I'll deduce where you were from a list of possibilities in my head.
jokes.setup: Marge asking where Homer's been
jokes.punchline: Homer's pseudo-scientific approach to avoiding conversation
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific Homer logic for avoiding normal interaction

jokes.text: Don't you usually worries about stuff? Do I?
jokes.setup: Homer pointing out Marge usually worries
jokes.punchline: Marge's uncertain response about her own personality
jokes.explanation: Good role reversal moment, Marge's self-doubt

jokes.text: Help me, mayonnaise!
jokes.setup: Homer realizing he has to worry about the kids
jokes.punchline: Appeals to condiment for help
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Homer moment, brilliant randomness, highly quotable

jokes.text: A thousand dollars?! That's what my house is worth!
jokes.setup: Teacher announcing $1000 prize for balsa wood contest
jokes.punchline: Homer equating prize to his house value
jokes.explanation: Good poverty humor, Homer's perspective on money

jokes.text: Too obvious. Got to be something from Earth.
jokes.setup: Homer planning Bart's balsa wood project
jokes.punchline: Rejecting space-based ideas for 'Earth' ones
jokes.explanation: Homer's flawed creative process, absurd constraint logic

jokes.text: I did it! I helped my son! Helicopter Homer away!
jokes.setup: Homer taking over Bart's project
jokes.punchline: Celebrating helicopter parenting with superhero catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Good callback to helicopter theme, Homer's misguided pride

jokes.text: Black Hawk down! Black Hawk down!
jokes.setup: Homer's 'Helicopter Homer away!' celebration
jokes.punchline: Immediately crashes with military helicopter reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect physical comedy timing, helicopter metaphor extended

jokes.text: Sweetie, our country was founded by a clique: the Continental Congress. Dolphins swim in cliques.
jokes.setup: Lisa resisting joining a clique
jokes.punchline: Marge's historical and biological justifications for cliques
jokes.explanation: Creative historical parallel, absurd dolphin fact, good Marge logic

jokes.text: It takes a lot of courage to wear suspenders when you're not in the circus.
jokes.setup: Demonstrating 'un-sults' technique from book
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment about suspenders
jokes.explanation: Good example of passive-aggressive technique, specific and mean

jokes.text: You saying my clothes are clown-like? I feel so insecure. Please be my friend.
jokes.setup: Rat receiving the suspenders 'un-sult'
jokes.punchline: Immediate emotional breakdown and desperate friendship request
jokes.explanation: Good demonstration of psychological manipulation effectiveness

jokes.text: There's also envy-tations, hate-hugs, spamming with faint praise
jokes.setup: Marge explaining more manipulation techniques from book
jokes.punchline: Series of portmanteau words for mean-girl tactics
jokes.explanation: Creative wordplay naming social manipulation tactics

jokes.text: I didn't mean 'everyone' everyone.
jokes.setup: Girl inviting 'everyone' to get frozen yogurt
jokes.punchline: Immediately excluding Lisa from 'everyone'
jokes.explanation: Classic mean-girl exclusion tactic, realistic cruelty

jokes.text: And that, my dear girl, is called the Toledo Take-back.
jokes.setup: After witnessing the yogurt exclusion
jokes.punchline: Naming the tactic with fake official terminology
jokes.explanation: Brilliant made-up term with geographical specificity

jokes.text: I'm a big girl! I'm a big girl!
jokes.setup: Marge's reverse psychology about the book
jokes.punchline: Lisa's immediate desperate response proving the manipulation worked
jokes.explanation: Perfect demonstration of manipulation success, Lisa's vulnerability

jokes.text: Son, you buy an awful lot of airplane glue, but you never buy any models.
jokes.setup: Store owner talking to Bart about purchasing patterns
jokes.punchline: Implication that Bart is huffing glue instead of building models
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about substance abuse, good observational setup

jokes.text: It's like saying, 'I don't care. My kid's a loser.'
jokes.setup: Criticizing Washington Monument as model choice
jokes.punchline: Brutal interpretation of what the choice means about parenting
jokes.explanation: Harsh educational judgment, good escalation of stakes

jokes.text: You've got to be crazy! Prepare to be frustrated! You won't get past the north transept!
jokes.setup: Homer announcing Westminster Abbey project
jokes.punchline: Parents' specific architectural warnings
jokes.explanation: Funny architectural specificity, competitive parent culture

jokes.text: Blabby's gonna build an Abbey. Groovy.
jokes.setup: Homer announcing Westminster Abbey project
jokes.punchline: Bart's dismissive rhyme and dated slang
jokes.explanation: Good Bart dismissal with wordplay, period slang humor

jokes.text: 'Ant poison'? Why do I always read the labels after?
jokes.setup: Marge applying spray-on tan for Lisa's party
jokes.punchline: Realizes she used ant poison instead
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup, Marge's domestic mishaps

jokes.text: Everything to make those hundred-dollar phones look like five-dollar toys.
jokes.setup: Marge describing cell phone decorating supplies
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the ironic devaluing effect of decoration
jokes.explanation: Sharp economic observation about consumer culture

jokes.text: I've never been called 'fierce' before. 'Strident.' 'Hectoring' has been tossed around.
jokes.setup: Girl complimenting Lisa as 'fierce'
jokes.punchline: Lisa's self-aware comparison to her usual descriptors
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa self-awareness, sophisticated vocabulary humor

jokes.text: A little further back. Do you want me to watch and learn? Naw, that kind of creeps me out.
jokes.setup: Homer taking over Bart's project, positioning Bart
jokes.punchline: Homer finds his own son watching him creepy
jokes.explanation: Good Homer weirdness, helicopter parenting taken to absurd extreme

jokes.text: That's not Henry the Seventh, it's Henry the Sixth! Stupid, stupid, stupid!
jokes.setup: Homer obsessively working on Westminster Abbey model
jokes.punchline: Homer's perfectionist meltdown over historical accuracy
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's obsessive behavior, historical detail

jokes.text: Geoffrey Chaucer?! What are you doing here? Well, I'm buried here.
jokes.setup: Homer's exhaustion causing hallucinations
jokes.punchline: Chaucer's matter-of-fact explanation about being buried at Westminster
jokes.explanation: Brilliant historical accuracy in hallucination, deadpan ghost logic

jokes.text: There are only two tragedies in life: one is not getting what one wants, and the other is getting it.
jokes.setup: Oscar Wilde appearing in Homer's hallucination
jokes.punchline: Famous Wilde quote about life's paradoxes
jokes.explanation: Actual Oscar Wilde quote, lower originality but high literary value

jokes.text: Experience is simply the name we give to our mistakes.
jokes.setup: Continuing Oscar Wilde's philosophical commentary
jokes.punchline: Another famous Wilde aphorism
jokes.explanation: Another actual Wilde quote, works thematically but not original

jokes.text: Whatever happened to 'boo?'
jokes.setup: Homer overwhelmed by Wilde's complex aphorisms
jokes.punchline: Homer's preference for simple ghost behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer response to intellectualism, brilliant simplicity

jokes.text: Now we could smash the real Westminster Abbey it looks like this. There are no flights to London till 5:00.
jokes.setup: Homer's pride in his detailed model
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual suggestion to destroy the real Abbey
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation to vandalism, Bart's casual destructiveness

jokes.text: He said your show. I watched it once!
jokes.setup: Reference to reality TV show format
jokes.punchline: Character's defensive response about watching trashy TV
jokes.explanation: Meta TV reference, character embarrassment about guilty pleasure

jokes.text: The only entry that repels my eye enough to be constructed by a student is this one.
jokes.setup: Judge evaluating obviously parent-made models
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment to actual student work
jokes.explanation: Good ironic judging, 'repels my eye' is perfect phrasing

jokes.text: In your face, losers! This helicopter is taking off!
jokes.setup: Bart wins by following rules while Homer's model loses
jokes.punchline: Homer's misguided victory celebration with helicopter metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good callback to helicopter theme, Homer missing the point

jokes.text: I didn't make this terrible model. My dad did.
jokes.setup: Bart winning the contest
jokes.punchline: Bart's honesty about his dad making the losing model
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart honesty, calling his dad's work terrible

jokes.text: 'I H-8 this?'
jokes.setup: Lisa reading message on decorated phone
jokes.punchline: Text-speak 'hate' message using number substitution
jokes.explanation: Standard text-speak joke but fits the mean-girl context

jokes.text: These girls are nice on the surface, but it's hard work staying this shallow.
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining why she's leaving the popular group
jokes.punchline: Paradox of working hard to maintain shallowness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant paradox about shallow behavior requiring effort, perfect Lisa insight

jokes.text: A light bulb is either on or it's off. Not if you use a dimmer switch. That's what the dimmer switch companies want you to think.
jokes.setup: Marge suggesting middle ground in parenting
jokes.punchline: Homer's conspiracy theory about dimmer switches
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic, brilliant corporate conspiracy about basic technology

jokes.text: The mattress the dog sleeps on in the cellar? No, I gave that to the homeless shelter. It was stuffed with cash!
jokes.setup: Homer guessing what Marge is referring to for relaxation
jokes.punchline: Reveal about accidentally donating cash-stuffed mattress
jokes.explanation: Great escalating absurdity, perfect Homer financial obliviousness

jokes.text: I feel like I've died and gone to Hell.
jokes.setup: Homer enjoying the sauna that's supposed to be relaxing
jokes.punchline: Describing pleasant experience as hellish
jokes.explanation: Standard ironic reversal but fits Homer's character perspective

tmdb_overview: Bart and Lisa start struggling in their academic and social lives at school and Homer starts monitoring them and forcing them to do better. Meanwhile, Marge discovers a sauna in the basement.
